executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in a single 48-hour period. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 21 victims, followed by Play (13), Safepay (11), and Clop (11). High-profile targets include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and the Municipality of North Perth. Critical infrastructure sectors hit hardest include healthcare, education, financial services, and public sector entities. This coordinated surge suggests either a mass exploitation campaign or delayed public disclosure of existing breaches.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information) - Double extortion ransomware with data leak sites

items.name: Play
items.targets: 13 Canadian victims including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products of Canada, Makivik, ALLMAX, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Clop

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a severe ransomware epidemic with 103 organizations publicly breached in 48 hours across all provinces. Critical sectors compromised include healthcare (Peterborough Public Health, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Bais Yaakov school), municipal governments (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer brand), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Kids & Company (childcare services) confirmed as victims. No major grocery or pharmacy chains (Loblaws, Shoppers Drug Mart, Canadian Tire, SportChek) identified.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked), Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash, Axion50plus, multiple CPA firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Reidhurst Nagy) all breached. No major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) directly identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical Linux kernel vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431, 'Copy Fail') is now actively exploited in the wild, enabling local privilege escalation on systems vulnerable for nearly a decade. The Canadian government's CCCS has issued three urgent advisories in the past week covering this Linux flaw, a critical cPanel vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) weaponized against government and MSP networks, and a warning about social engineering attacks on enterprise cloud environments. ShinyHunters threat actor has claimed breaches of Canvas/Instructure (second breach in under a year) and credentials are being stolen via DigiCert certificate compromise. Canadian organizations face elevated risk from the cPanel exploit and SaaS compromise campaigns.

items.description: Linux kernel 'Copy Fail' flaw enabling local privilege escalation, present since 2017
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High (root access)

items.description: Critical cPanel and WebHost Manager authentication bypass leading to admin access
items.software_affected: cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM), 40,000+ servers affected
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High (admin takeover)

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Legacy vulnerability being scanned by threat actors from Kazakhstan IP
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Canvas/Instructure (educational technology platform, second breach in under a year)
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), data exfiltration and underground marketplace sale

items.name: Silver Fox
items.targets: Organizations in Russia and India (government, retail, energy sectors)
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), tax-themed phishing lures, ABCDoor backdoor deployment

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: City of Sandstone
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion ransomware

items.name: DragonForce (ransomware group)
items.targets: Cult Wines
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: MuddyWater (APT, Iran-aligned)
items.targets: Microsoft 365 environments, global espionage campaigns
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1110 (Brute Force), credential stuffing attacks against weak M365 credentials

items.name: CyberAv3ngers (Iranian-affiliated)
items.targets: Internet-exposed PLCs in the United States
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), ICS/SCADA exploitation

items.name: 313 Team (pro-Iran hacktivist)
items.targets: Canonical (during Ubuntu 26 launch), extortive DDoS attacks
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1491 (Defacement), extortion

items.name: Salt Typhoon (suspected)
items.targets: IBM subsidiary managing Italy's public administration infrastructure
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), long-term espionage, persistence for 2 weeks

items.name: AccountDumpling (phishing campaign)
items.targets: 30,000+ Facebook accounts compromised via Google-sent phishing wave
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), session hijacking, 2FA bypass

items.name: Mnt6 (ransomware group)
items.targets: McKay electrical contractor (New Zealand)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), January 2026 breach confirmed

items.name: Unknown (SMS blasters)
items.targets: Canadian streets, mass device compromise via SMS interception
items.ttps: T1659 (Content Injection), T1430 (Location Tracking), physical proximity attacks with SMS blasting equipment

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued three critical advisories this week: CVE-2026-31431 (Linux kernel privilege escalation actively exploited), CVE-2026-41940 (cPanel zero-day targeting government and MSP networks with 40,000+ servers affected), and a warning about social engineering compromise of enterprise SaaS environments. Threat actors physically deployed SMS blasting equipment on Canadian streets to compromise devices. A Toronto Police division involved in Project South has a documented history of data breaches.
